channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. — 173 replies — 692.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low.

Open thread • Replies: 173 • Updated: 2026-06-09 13:56 UTC

OP

At this point the probability of the LINK being needed is low. Logically thinking there would be at least SOME buying pressure from other people in the know and price would be held up at key points with traces of bullish absorption in the chart yet theres none of that just a fast move up into a slow bleed. If you do any type of chart watching you would know exactly what im talking about. With just a glance at the LINK chart you can tell there is not a single good/bullish thing about it.

You can't convince me people involved in these announcements with chainlink wouldn't buy some themselves and talk to their other high-class or wealthy friends and those people would buy and word would continue to spread. It just doesnt make sense unless.... the people with inside knowledge know LINK isnt necessary for the services chainlink provides.

Call me a faggot call me indian call me fudder call me retard but it just doesnt make ANY sense despite all of these announcements and the apparent fact that these giant companies are using chainlink that the LINK does not trend upward.

I dont know why im posting this I guess I just wanted to get some thoughts from you guys or rant into the void. Ive been in since 2021 and bought around 15 dollars. I'm not sure if I can hold much longer and on the other hand I dont know what I would even buy even if I sold. I wish I could invest in the company Chainlink.

Early replies (first 3)

62312631
How big is your stack

62312653
>LINK starts to moon in sats
>fuddies get riled up
Like clockwork

62312685
>>62312653
ironically this is exactly how they get away with their accumulation. either during btc dips or OTC buys. meanwhile retail looks at the price, demoralization continues, they continue to accumulate.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62330274
>>62328534
>That's what I said you said.
Devil's in the details; not the same thing and it's why your BTC comparison doesn't work.

>>>miners decide on the rate of BTC being mined via the difficulty and the reward per block
>That's completely false.
Yes anon, there is a consensus for the reward per block. The miners are included in this consensus.

62331087
>>62330274
>not the same thing
It's the same thing.

>there is a consensus for the reward per block. The miners are included in this consensus.
You went from "miners decide" to "miners are part of the decision".

The reality is miners can't decide shit. Even if all miners are 100% in consensus among themselves, all they can do is ask the market to also agree with them. Which is something anyone with a twitter account can do.

62331850
>>62312653
LINK holders are the liberals of crypto, you're completely delusional.
You're on your way out though.

62332143
>>62331087
if miners stop the difficulty plummets
if miners want to support a fork they can redirect all the hashrate
are you legitimately retarded?

62332425
>>62332143
>if miners stop the difficulty plummets
Yes.
And the lower the lower the mining difficulty, the more smaller mining rigs can mine.

>if miners want to support a fork they can redirect all the hashrate
Yes, they'll redirect all their hashrate to a fork nobody will use.

You changed IDs for this? You fucking goofball.

62332438
All of you need to KYS’sselves

62332450
>>62332425
>miners can't decide shit
>eerrr they can but they won't
I accept your concession, keep moving the goalposts you fucking imbecile

62332473
>>62332450
>>eerrr they can but they won't

kek what the fuck are you talking about?

62332491
glad the chainlink general thread format is finding acceptance

62332578
>>62332491
You keep saying this, but it's pretty much just fuddies creating about 20 fudthreads and then bumping one ad nauseum. The amount of link fud threads on the board doesn't decrease because of this "general" at all. Maybe mods should just ban all LINK fud threads and corral them into a single fuddie general, it's beyond ridiculous now that the board is so slow.

62333330
>>62325222
Chainlink confidential compute for private tokenization too

62335182
>>62332578
shut up cuckold

price + chart :)

62335458
>>62335182
Thanks for bumping /CFG/ (Chainlink Fuddie General)

62336610
>>62332425
>You changed IDs for this? You fucking goofball.
Still here. Feel free to also refute my point on your BTC comparison falling apart due to the flow of change of hands from new BTC to node operators to market versus new Link tokens going from CLL's uncirculated supply directly to Binance instead of node operators.

62336627
>>62335458
p+c bitch

62336936
>>62316605
>Chainlink pays the nodes from their own team wallets

>>62336610
>Link tokens don't go from CLL's uncirculated supply to node operators

You're a fucking retard you know that.

62337435
>>62333330
Epstein should have stored his blackmail material in here amirite

62337475
If chainlink is decentralized why do not even the biggest, stinkiest linkies here brag about running a node?

62338077
>>62337475
linkpool made us think we could back in the day, it was all a scam, we all had a running node setup and we were waiting to be whitelisted, what a fking joke

62340215
>>62336936
You're misrepresenting my point to argue something else. Why are you doing this? You get nothing out of it unless you're a paid shill or just bored.

62340223
>>62337475
We tried. It's a full time job requiring a bunch of resources on top of the profits being nebulous thus souring a lot of people out of running their node [on AWS]. This was back in 2019 so I don't know how robust and improved running nodes is nowadays.

62340231
>>62340215
I argued against your point that "Chainlink paying the nodes from their own team wallets" decouples the link price from the usage; and then you literally asked me to refute your point that Chainlink doesn't pay the nodes from the uncirculating supply, and is instead sending the Link directly to Binance.

What the fuck are you even doing here?

62340287
>>62340231
> BTC
> No new BTC enters the market except through miners' hands.
> This means they decide and influence the price of BTC.
> This couples the BTC price to the usage of the network because miners will not sell less than the costs of operations

> Link
> new Link enters the market through payments to node operators, payments to developers, and to deals with companies
> Link node operators are no longer the sole arbiters who decide when new Link enters the market
> To access services from the network, you don't need to pay in Link, and your payments made aren't immediately converted to Link, if at all. This decouples the link token from accurately reflecting network activity
> Furthermore CLL has routine token unlocks and will also move tokens to a centralized exchange to capitalize on market movements, Ex: China's response to the Trump tariffs

https://etherscan.io/tx/0x6e347aa07b74b2330b8f3f5a9da44305cba0bf7e40b416bcf00ba9922c365fdd
https://etherscan.io/tx/0x3355d51eafaead23e063aba8a099a712a11ecb26e937f53d0850cd4c5b33801e
https://etherscan.io/tx/0x2e3b0111c94d9d0102e5822ccd9dc5f395c5b207b39a466432c1d634c4354eb6

^Above transactions took place just an hour before the great crash of October


Your job is to refute my conclusion that the link token price is NOT tied to network usage. Refute these points. You are free to disregard the BTC comparison since those are points in my favor unless you can refute my points. Any thing other than directly addressing and showcasing flaws in my logic (and I better hear something better than the low hanging fruit of "Node operators have to sell Link for cost operations too!") and I will respond with "token not needed".

62340304
>>62340287
You specifically made the point that the Link that does go through the node operators decouples the link price from the usage.
This is what I refuted.
I pointed this out to you many times but you keep pretending not to understand or something.

62340325
>>62340304
>You specifically made the point that the Link that does go through the node operators decouples the link price from the usage.

token not sneeded

62340347
>>62340325
>>62340287
>CLL has routine token unlocks and will also move tokens to a centralized exchange to capitalize on market movements
This is horseshit by the way.
He has always sold on Fridays, pretty much around the same hour, and always within 3 to 4 month intervals.

Sometimes Link pumps right after (many times in fact), sometimes some shit happens right after. But his timing is fully transparent.

62340403
>>62340347
Anon, the etherscan links posted here >>62340287 are the transactions Sergey made on the tip of what Binance was going to do in response to Trump's tariffs. For the uninitiated, Trump proposed a 100% tariff on China and China responded with crashing the market. You can dig into the archives to verify. Type in either "Trump", "China" "Binance", "Link" or whatever and date it to October.

62340410
Fuck you chainlinkies!

Sincerely,

The whole XRP community

62340414
>>62340403
>the etherscan links posted here >>62340287 are the transactions Sergey made on the tip of what Binance was going to do
Those links are the transactions Sergey made on a Friday after a 3 to 4 month interval.
Which has been his unlocking regimen since forever.

62340536
>>62340287
If (almost) no one uses the network, will the token price change or remain (almost) unchanged?

item.title: Chainlinkies are the ones that try to make fun of XRP chads by spamming the boom-meme. — 13 replies — 45.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Chainlinkies are the ones that try to make fun of XRP chads by spamming the boom-meme.

Open thread • Replies: 13 • Updated: 2026-06-09 13:53 UTC

OP

Chainlinkies are the ones that try to make fun of XRP chads by spamming the boom-meme. Look at this poster who copies and pastes an entire thread just to shit it up and make fun of XRP holders: >>62338460

The funny thing? He forgot to remove the (You) when he copied and pasted the whole thing. But you can see his ID is different.

So my best guess is it's probably a single poster who constantly switches id to force this shitty meme. Probably a chainlink bagholder.

Early replies (first 3)

62339778
Imagine caring this much, holy shit. Adult bully victim no doubt

62339781
>>62339778
Lol of course I don't care so much but it goes to show how pathetic those link tards are trying to force that unfunny boom-xrp meme. The guy thinks if he switches id it looks like more people are in on it but that retard forgot to remove the (You) from his own post.

You are just butthurt you got called out hard and i'm gonna show this to everyone on /biz/ so they know who is really responsible for forcing that dumb meme.

62339829
>>62339778
idk bout that... xrp apparently lives rent free in linkbaggies minds

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62339835
>>62339829
100%
And I caught him red handed

And Chainlinkies be all like: "Durrr imagine caring this isn't important"

62340175
>>62339844
Xrp hasn't reclaimed it's all time high after a whole decade. If xrp baggies didn't sell they have blown their chance of limiting their losses and buying something useful

Kaboom

62340182
>>62339768
Its biepum the yeet node operator
Give him a break, chainlink shilling and xrp fudding is his personality

62340345
>>62339768
so fxcking bullish

62340350
Boooomers and peelers won

62340365
>>62339768
>Chainlinkies are the ones that try to make fun of XRP chads by spamming the boom-meme.
GIGABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!

62340374
>>62339768
Why would anyone buy Chainlink or xrp I don't understand. Crypto is there to buy drugs online. Can you buy drugs online with Chainlink ? No. Can you buy drugs online with xrp? No. Case closed.

62340393
>>62339768
I don't hold link either tho
KABOOOOOOOOOOOM

62340516
everybody hates u dumb stinks

item.title: This fucker sold 32 BTC, only to buy 1,550 more a few days ago. — 8 replies — 19.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
This fucker sold 32 BTC, only to buy 1,550 more a few days ago.

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-06-09 13:45 UTC

OP

This fucker sold 32 BTC, only to buy 1,550 more a few days ago.
This con man isn’t liquidating shit, he’s buying up all the BTC for cheap the little weasel

Early replies (first 3)

62339800
fuck saylor

62339854
>>62339793

62339857
Saylor is a cuck. It will go lower.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62339868
>>62339857
Depends by what measure. Cost of energy to mine a BTC is right at $60,000.
This could be an obvious bottom right before Clarity act passes and billions flow in from ETFs

62339965
How tf is blud's average buying price 75k
This nigga was already accumulating when btc was below 20k
He's even worse at investing than me

62340052
>>62339965
picrel n sheeeit.
he really is a cretin who shouldn't be allowed out unaccompanied in public.

62340351
>>62340052
Why sell 32 BTC and immediately buy 1,550 a few days later? He manufactured fear and drove the price lower to buy cheaper.
I truly believe Clarity is going to pass soon, that or something bullish for crypto. Or perhaps Craig is simply corrupt, idk

62340483
Imagine DCAing for 6 years for an $11 billion loss (not adjusted for inflation, wages and the lack of liquidity)

item.title: MICROPLASTIC REMOVER SOFT IP — 2 replies — 8.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
MICROPLASTIC REMOVER SOFT IP

Open thread • Replies: 2 • Updated: 2026-06-09 14:03 UTC

OP

Okay, Gemini and I solved Microplastics, get the FUCK in here, Silicon Valley Geeks and get that unicorn running! Endless revenue from post-Operation super grassroots to establish turned into recreational AI vibrating float tanks

>In essence, provide a medicine that creates "fizzyness" in organs to dislodge microplastics from organs
>[GEMINI]: You engineer a biocompatible, hydrophobic lipid or liquid nano-emulsion. Because it "hates" water just as much as the plastic does, it will naturally bypass healthy, water-rich tissue and seek out the exact nanocreases where the microplastics are hiding.
>Then you use an AI tuned and trained to deliver highly specialised vibrations in the water to push out the microplastics, this solves the acoustic coupling problem.
>[GEMINI]: Uniform Wave Propagation: Water and the human body have almost identical densities. Because the patient is floating, transducer speakers built into the tank walls can pump precise, low-frequency acoustic vibrations through the water. These waves pass cleanly into the body with zero resistance.
>The Full-Body "Fizz": Instead of a clinical ultrasound machine blasting one tiny area, the entire body experiences a gentle, uniform hum. If the patient has ingested the hydrophobic compound that coats the plastic, these full-body vibrations trigger that microscopic "fizz" simultaneously in the liver, kidneys, blood vessels, and bladder.
2. The Tech: What the AI Does
>The AI isn't just a marketing buzzword here; it is mathematically required to prevent the acoustic waves from tearing healthy tissue.
Every human body has a different mass, bone density, and fat-to-muscle ratio. If you blast the exact same acoustic frequency into a 120-pound person and a 250-pound person, the waves will bounce around differently, potentially causing painful hotspots or completely missing target organs.

Early replies (first 3)

62340507
>Finally, redumping the urine with dense nanoparticles is counterproductive. How can we treat the urine chemically postop for clumping of nanoparticles into macroparticles and polymer reformation into better chains?
>[GEMINI]: To solve this, the treatment shouldn't happen at the city sewage plant—it has to happen inside the clinic's collection plumbing or right at the toilet bowl level immediately "post-op."
By treating the urine chemically the second it leaves the patient, you can force the nanoparticles to clump (flocculate) and bind the loose plastic fragments back into macro-scale polymer chains that are easy to physically filter out.
>Clump, or "floc" the nanoparticles into larger clumps, modify the polymer chains, and now you have bricks of high density plastic for "tanks" as Gemini puts it
>After global op is done, business can semi retired as recreational vibrating float tanks

https://g.co/gemini/share/1d86fd8428c2

62340571
>>62340501

Would you volunteer for being the patient zero?

I would love to fuck your body with those cozy vibe coded vibrations.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

No additional replies in the latest window.

item.title: QQQ vs. VOO / AKA Not Squandering a Bull Run While Young — 5 replies — 5.1/hr
item.content:encoded:
QQQ vs. VOO / AKA Not Squandering a Bull Run While Young

Open thread • Replies: 5 • Updated: 2026-06-09 13:11 UTC

OP

>Been DCA into VOO for a fair bit now
>Realize I shouldn't squander this bull run and at least take an Index / ETF with more volatility if I don't wanna bet on individual stocks like Sandisk or Nvidia
>Look at QQQ vs VOO real returns over time
>QQQ and VOO just mirror each other, with some years performing the same, otherwise QQQ has almost exactly twice the gains and twice the loses (by % not year count) as VOO
As someone who is only 26, why would I not just go into the more volatile option here that still gives more returns
Is QQQ way too tech focused right now, not that VOO isn't tech focused, but I heard the Nasaq was bending the rules to allow these AI companies in

Please reply with actual advice, analysis, your own portfolios as examples, alternatives to these two without betting the farm, etc. I just wanna DCA my paychecks into shit without feeling like I missed the boat or FOMO (not that I have that much)

Much appreciated.

Early replies (first 3)

62338683
>>62338660
you already missed a lot of the difference. That said I'm still buying QQQ rather than other index funds bc everyone thinks we're already at the top of the tech bubble. Also don't make the noob mistake QQQM is technically better for longterm holding but it's marginal really

62338807
>>62338660
>only 26
You should be going deep on 0DTE 20% OTM calls on NVDA earnings

62338873
>>62338807
why chase premiums? legit, why
>>62338683
sadly just wasn't fully employed until a year and a half ago.....

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62339090
Bump

62340367
QQQ is going to tilt you in the direction of the tech boom
VOO also includes you in this since they’re on the S&P anyway
It’s more growth but more volatility
QQQ is just a bias towards growth and VOO is a bias towards total market exposure
As long as those top companies remain heavily skewed in their profitability then go with QQQ
Or go with both
VOO and chill exists for a reason then it’s probably better like look into other ETFs that focus on a particular sector if you wanted more growth

item.title: Unstaking my LINK in ~5 hours or so. I have a bit over 8,500 staked between my wallet and my dad's mini wallet, I hope o… — 46 replies — 24.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Unstaking my LINK in ~5 hours or so. I have a bit over 8,500 staked between my wallet and my dad's mini wallet, I hope o…

Open thread • Replies: 46 • Updated: 2026-06-09 12:17 UTC

OP

Unstaking my LINK in ~5 hours or so. I have a bit over 8,500 staked between my wallet and my dad's mini wallet, I hope one of you anons gets my spot!

Early replies (first 3)

62333114
>>62332979
Congrats. You'll be able to buy back triple your stack when it goes to $2

62333117
>>62332979
Aaaaaa anon u buddy basterd, I am travelling and won't get in

62333146
>>62332979
can u partially unstake?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62333297
>>62333271
And it takes 30 days I’m pretty sure for the pool to actually open up and him get his tokens back.

62333303
>>62333297
Anon, did you not look at my screenshot? I have waited the 30 days. I have a few hours left.

62333306
>>62333297
Disregard just saw the cooldown

62333312
>>62333288
Pocket change sells

62333345
wait
>file 177
>not a screenie
>he saved pic from another thread
>another larp
>awww

62333369
>>62333345
>>he saved pic from another thread
>anon is so stupid he can't see timestamps on posts
Are all LINK cultists this retarded?

62333482
>>62332979
Wait linkcucks stake for only a 4% return? Seriously??

62333807
Godspeed anon, where do you intend to put the money, out of crypto or?

62333867
>>62333288
Timed it well

62334250
Anyone standing by to take my spot?

62334269
>>62334250
Everyone sold their link

62334387
>>62334250
Kind of you sir
Already got 15k staked

62334454
wow what a surprise op didn't follow through

62334757
>>62332979
thanks for staking OP!

62334878
>>62333181
ywnbaw

62334890
>>62332979
Lmao this board to me is just proof of a demiurge LINK should be trading at over $100 a token but I'm sure it will go back down to $3.50

62335438
>>62332979
you were one of our strongest bag holders
we'll never forget you

62335486
>>62333217
everyone laugh at this pink cuck

62335879
sooooo OP... didnt follow through?? kek what a bitch

62335931
>>62335879
He did but it was filled instantly, again

62336009
>>62335931
what is the contract address? i want to check the transactions
not only OPs but actual movement, and see how fast spots get sniped

62336583
>>62334250
You are a good man for attempting to coordinate to help other anons get in.

62336591
>>62336009
It'll likely be by the second since stake.link is now sniping every opening as opposed to ignoring unstaked token windows under 300 or something.

https://etherscan.io/token/0x514910771af9ca656af840dff83e8264ecf986ca?a=0xbc10f2e862ed4502144c7d632a3459f49dfcdb5e#transactions

62336787
>>62336591
They upped it to 300? Nice they had it at 50 before. Might get around to restake some rewards

62337490
>>62334890
its overvalued by at least 10x, if not 100x

62340150
Did you even do it OP?

62340207
>>62336787
You have it the other way around; they lowered it from 300. 300 or 500 was the window where it would snipe a spot back when staking first came out. Now, it's any window; meaning you can't stake manually, you need to write a bot.

62340218
>>62340207
I'm quite sure they are not sniping spots below 50 LINK, look at the history I can't find any Deposit Queued Tokens method call where the total triggering Unstaked amount is smaller than 50

62340230
>>62340218
Okay, I was incorrect on it, so it's just 50. Cool cool cool. So you somewhat have a chance to manually unstake.

item.title: No regrets selling BTC?? — 15 replies — 23.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
No regrets selling BTC??

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-06-09 13:32 UTC

OP

Some talk about regretting it.

Anyone sold and still thinks it was the right move?

If you had the cash from that sale sitting in your account today, would you buy BTC again? Because I definitely would.

Early replies (first 3)

62339349
I sold my last bitcoin last year. Shitcoin is dead. AI is dead. Time to start acting like an african american.

62339372
>>62339349
Maybe regrets for buying? Should have bought chainlink

62339382
I rotated into XMR at like $105k, no regrets at all.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62339395
>>62339382
Why monero?

62339401
>>62339328
>Anyone buying ICP?

62339417
>>62339395
why monero? people actually use it, the fact that bitcoin is transparent is actually quite a con in itself. stablecoins, ETH and its L2s (think polygon, defi, smart contracts etc) and privacy coins have genuine utility; something like HYPE has utility.

Anything with bitcoin it's just about its "heckin price" that's all it is, nobody uses it.

62339452
>>62339417
>Nobody uses Bitcoin
Bro… The largest world conflict at this very moment involves Bitcoin.

62339462
>>62339452
>2 months ago

62339510
>>62339328
I don't feel bad, I sold near 100k, 120k and 100k again to buy this dip in stocks and especially gold.
I will be rebuying BTC lower starting August.

62339716
>>62339349
Thanks for your sacrifice. The bull market appreciates your contribution.

62339730
>>62339328

No regrets here. I did have over 2BTC back in 2013-2014 and if i had just decided to hold, it would've all been gone now because the exchange they were on (BTC-E) got shut down by the DOJ and unlike with Mt.Gox, users pretty much got shafted.

You can't just pick and choose ideal past moments from life to do differently, that's not how shit works. If you had that kind of clairvoyance you could easily just win the lottery or just time the current market perfectly as if you're insider-trading to become rich within a matter of weeks.

62339878
>>62339372
Why did you regret buying?

62339883
>>62339328
Sold it with no regrets, tripled my initial investment, moved it into stocks which actually move up
Some posters laugh at boomer stocks but btc and most crypto has been going down for well over a year, even shit boomer stocks that barely beat inflation are better, at this point crypto is dead and the days of it doing massive 10x in a day will never happen again

62340066
>>62339328
>Anyone sold and still thinks it was the right move?
sold 0.3 above 90k, still have 1.7 left.
i hve 31k to buy back in october.

i also sold 0.6 below 40k and i do regret that.

62340438
>>62339382
What's your price goal for xmr?

item.title: How do you actually cash out? (a large amount) — 41 replies — 18.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
How do you actually cash out? (a large amount)

Open thread • Replies: 41 • Updated: 2026-06-09 11:58 UTC

OP

I'm planning on selling a decent chunk of my stack next year in order to pay for house construction.

Has anyone sold a large amount OTC and had the funds deposited in a US or European bank account? Any tips?

I'm talking 6 to 7 figures

Early replies (first 3)

62336656
>>62336622
cash out?

62336664
>>62336622
You talk to a tax consultant and let your bank know in advance that you have big crypto deposits coming in and to not flag your account

62336685
>>62336664
thanks, but how do you convert the crypto in the first place? OTC company, crypto exchange, something else?

do you know if some banks are more tolerant than others? i have accounts in the US and Europe

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62336853
>>62336798
I've had the same Coinbase account since 2017 already. This is were the money to crypto first came in and now over the last few years all went out to my bank again.

For tax purposes I've been tracking my crypto with Koinly for the last ~4 years. Maybe about 10 exchanges and 10 wallets, with multiple chains and DeFi trades also. Was not too difficult even. Havent been able to trace everything retroactively perfectly but good enough.

The bank eventually also wanted to have some documents of my transactions. To make sure I am not connected to some blacklisted addresses or that no other money came into my accounts than my own. This is thousands of transactions/buys/sells were talking about. I gave them documents that Koinly can generate. I kind of doubt the bank people can understand it all, but they are satisfied to have something and sense I am not trying to hide anything.

62336859
You don't have enough to sell OTC. You sell to the orderbook.

62336873
>>62336622
SI-SIX SEVEN!!

62336892
>>62336664
Basically this
Unless you're explicitly trying to dodge taxes, which you can't do past cashing out above a certain amount, there's no issues in selling crypto
All the horror stories around here are from dishonest scammers that aren't telling they got their crypto from very poor opsec crime

62336920
Oh yes, absolutely. Because nothing says "carefully planned home construction" quite like tying your entire building budget to one of the most volatile asset classes on Earth and then assuming every intermediary involved will roll out a red carpet while a mountain of money teleports into your bank account.
In this magnificent circus act, the market will obviously cooperate, your chosen exit date will coincide with peak prices, the OTC desk will operate with the speed of a Formula 1 pit crew, your bank's compliance department will greet the incoming wire with confetti cannons, and tax authorities will simply nod approvingly from a distance. What could possibly go wrong?
The fantasy gets even better. You're building a house, a project famous for never experiencing delays, cost overruns, contractor disputes, permit issues, material shortages, or surprise expenses. Pair that with an asset that can decide to impersonate a falling piano at any moment, and you've created the financial equivalent of juggling chainsaws while riding a unicycle across a tightrope made of spaghetti.
Then comes the banking adventure. People talk about large transfers as if they're ordering a pizza. In reality, moving significant sums often involves documentation, source-of-funds checks, compliance reviews, questions, more questions, and occasionally questions about the questions. Yet somehow the expectation is that a giant OTC sale will produce a magical wire transfer that glides through the global financial system like a bald eagle soaring through a sunset.
The best part is the assumption that next year will conveniently be the perfect time to sell. Not six months earlier. Not six months later. Exactly when the concrete needs pouring and the invoices arrive. That's not financial planning; that's consulting a crystal ball while wearing clown shoes.
By all means, have an exit strategy.

62337140
>>62336656
this reminded me of 2017 when crypto was being talked about in the mainstream now and people started asking about whether or not you could actually cash out of crypto lol

62337148
>>62336873
God bless your soul

62337230
>>62336892
the issue will be with the bank that will freeze the account and question you like a criminal. should be resolvable but still a pain in the ass and lets you know who really is the one in control

62337875
>>62336622
the Jews will never allow it

62337902
>>62336920
>an asset that can decide to impersonate a falling piano at any moment
made me chuckle

62337909
>>62336920
funny clanker

62337949
>>62336622
I had huge issues cashing out. Tried to sell on the same exchange where I bought years ago and they wouldn't let me. Beside all the kyc bullshit, had to prove the source of the initial funds, even though they let me buy with no questions asked back then. A nightmare.

62338205
>>62336763
Took out 100k in the summer via Kraken to Revolut. No questions asked at all

62338346
>>62338205
part of making it is triggering the automated unusual activity detections that lead to interrogations or account suspensions on those less caring about individual customers

62338356
>>62336664
I didn't even tell the bank. Just transferred $300K straight from coinbase. They didn't even call like they have for some other large deposits. I did let the accountant know upfront but only because I had to talk with him about some other stuff. If your bank gives you shit about having money then you should get another bank.

62338965
>>62336622
I cashed out mid 6 figure via coinbase, transfer to my regular local bank. It was literally nothing, I did go see bank branch manager and told the the deposit was Coming and wanted to no fuckery, he understood but also said it was literally no issue.
After I did this, they started and have continued to give me all my deposits from job immediately, they don't sit on them for days like they do to all the plebs.
I hate banks so much.

62338979
I cashed about 80k from coinbase, not the same as your numbers but zero issues here. Didn't tell anyone I was doing it, no one cared, I figured I am a small fry.

62338985
>>62336622
You can’t cash out. Every major CEX will freeze your account immediately when you try and you’ll lose your money. They’ve done it for less. The only way is use multiple Asian banks at 20k a piece. Don’t even risk sending more than 10k to goybase a year

62338990
>>62338985
Why do I always see these posts? Who makes them? Simply not true and I don't believe you've experienced this.

62339110
>>62338990
It's true tho. The bank just denies the transaction and it's unable to be recovered.

62339183
>>62336622
depends on the liquidity. if it's some shitcoin with crappy liquidity you either want to slowly swap or otc. if it's eth or something you should not even be worried about cashing out 6 to 7 figures. just find the exchange with the lowest fees, dex or cex. if you can swap it to usdc on a dex for less fees than selling on a cex you can then send it over to coinbase to cash out with no fee.

62339195
>>62339110
This. Do it piece by piece.

The banks know crypto will kill them, and they've paid regulators to go along with it.

They can just block the transaction, and the exchange won't give a fuck and say it's gone.

62339234
>>62336622
when you want to cash out, you switch to DAI or USDT on TRX. then you find someone you trust locally that wants to give you $ in person. that's it.

62339239
>>62336920
>that's not x, it's x
ai posters should be maimed and tortured

62339497
UHH GUYS I CAN'T CASH OUT

62339725
>>62339497
Don't worry. You are the kind of trader who doesnt need to cash out

62339749
>>62339110
>>62339195
>your money will just like dissapear
Again, 1pbtid making up shit..

62339794
>>62336622
>Any tips?
Trocador and Crypto ATMs.

62340092
>>62336622
Get a Uphold debit card and spend it through that. You get 2% back in crypto. If you pay $100 you will get 4% back in crypto instead which is worth it at that level of purchase. Or buy gold with it and collateralize the loan against it.

62340190
>>62336685
Obviously you need to sell for cash first. Don’t just place a $800k sell order unless it’s Bitcoin on Coinbase and even then I’d do 8 orders of $100k separately. Use the biggest exchange you can. You don’t need OTC for sub 7 figures.

62329297

62330860

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62334725

62335531
>>62328888

KABOOOOOOOOOM

62335674
>>62328888
Checked

Did they find the missing 32,569 blocks Schwarz “lost” and which totally didn’t grant him 8% of the supply?

62335685
>>62334725
Kaboooooom xrp $254 soon!

62335820
GIGABOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM!!!

62337017
>>62335820

62337474
>>62335674
For every block a fuddie posts here

62339287
>>62334725
BOOOOOOOOOOM!!!

62339380
>>62328888

Buy polkadot

62339618
>>62335674
Original blocks aren't relevant to XRP's consensus you retarded nigger. Not every network is proof of work/stake. Did they find the 95% of total supply of all existing eth that was given to the CCP (Wangxiang)/JP Morgan in the ICO which is never accounted into peoples' thinking about a PROOF OF STAKE network? Did they find the operator rewards Sirgay sold publicly to retail to make pay for his company's software business with the network being inadequate to support literally 1/4 of its functional existence on actual revenues from use?

62339628
>>62339618
BOOOOOOOOOOOOOOOM

62339675
>>62339618
so fxcking bullish xrp

62340044
>>62328888

>1k likes
>0 comments
Lol cripple marketing spam on here is mostly bots, so of course their yt vids are also botted

62340359
>>62340044

item.title: Alright fellas, I've been here for more years than I care to admit and although this place is an absolute stinker these… — 52 replies — 13.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
Alright fellas, I've been here for more years than I care to admit and although this place is an absolute stinker these…

Open thread • Replies: 52 • Updated: 2026-06-09 10:23 UTC

OP

Alright fellas, I've been here for more years than I care to admit and although this place is an absolute stinker these days I'm willing to give it one more change. This market sucks and everyones bored.

Why don't we pick a random ass coin with decent liq and asslow mc just like was once done with SHIB to name one and start shilling here and later x / tg. Everyone's bored and is looking for a quick buck, the market is waiting for something like this.

I've sat through SHIB, APU, HOGE, and various other 200x and still somehow not managed to become a millionaire and I'm getting pretty sick of it. I can't live like this. Let's pick a coin that ticks the boxes and push it as the next SHIB style moon. Let's start by finding the coin, I trust in you.

Early replies (first 3)

62332932
cheese

62332951
This has been tried a hundred times before and it never works out because this place is now filled with tourists and indians who'll fuck it up to lock in their 4 figs of profit

62332953
will never work. 99% of people that make these kinds of threads are part of the industrial token complex that has been fleecing retarded people on here for years

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62334516
>>62334406
>>62334451
just give up, baggies.
Shyoshi farmed and rugged all of you and he still has 20T more to dump on you.
even Ryoshi gave up on Shitba

F A R M E D

62334517
Toad killer is the official rich pedo token that will make millionaires this upcoming alt season

62334525
people shilling shitters that are years old are tonedeaf to the market. floki is the sole exception to the rule that old tokens never pump. it has to be somewhat new and ideally topical to the cultural zeigeist

and floki of course gave back all the gains after it had its miracle pump back to ath

62334656
Just buy and hold bitcoin, faggot. Stop giving your money away to Indian/eastern european dev scamfaggots

62334676
>>62334516
>Ryoshi gave up on Shitba
Good. Shib is healing.

62334733
>>62334013
MIRAI the WhiteRabbit
>eth
>mc currently under 20k
>clean tokensniff
>cute mascot
>the future will always be in vogue

62335122
>>62334203
The point is we bizbros buy in before the telegrams, x wimps and cuntfluencers do. Just like before, the market conditions are not necessarily a bad thing, in bull you have abundance of shitters now you have nothing really being shilled
>>62334248
Only ETH newfriend
>>62334406
We want something with pref sub 100k mc
>>62334461
It’s all a game and this is the only way to make it in this clown world, your stocks are more rubbish than my morning turd most likely
>>62334733
Name is clumsy and lame. Too little normie appeal with anime. No website.
It needs a website. Other than that not bad keep em coming

62335134
>>62334013
Good list of requirements I would add these:
>must have website and pref some social following
>must be under 100k mc, even as low as 10-20k would be perfect. That’s how you are able to gather momentum fast.

62335164
Sure smells like curry in here.
Have some free gas fee's
>BRAP!

62335171
Thread is gone from the catalog, wrap it up boys.
With that being said, Gondola is the answer.
CMC, Coin Gecko, Etherscan, Dexscreener all paid for and updated.
Website and TG still up and semi active.
Most of the original guys are gone, but a couple are left and keeping it alive.
X premium still being paid for, and the account posts daily.
Just under 100k MC.
Peaked at 12-14M MC, then had its knee caps lopped off when the trump coin BS happened.
Slowly crashed along with the entire market after that.
And unlike what that sol fag posted above, eth coins that make it tend to need a couple years before they really rip.
Gondola is about to hit its 2 year anniversary.

62335469
>>62334511
Also holdin elien on sol for same reason lul. Also down but idc
Actualy giga random that someone wrote elien.. thus it rly has its buyers. I mean when i heard the word instantly went on search as i knew its a Runner

62337794
>>62334511
>>62334733
>>62335171
Will check these ones out. I was also looking at Goblin on eth which recently had a cto because Sam Altman posted about goblins.

62337913
>>62337794
I think gondola or goblin would have the best chance to run unless Elon Musk is actually an alien.
>Gondola
Has the old meme appeal and doesn't really have competition. This is probably the best choice for op's goal out of everything in the thread I'd say.
>Goblin
Has a competitor on SOL which seems to be doing much better. It's about a year old and is trying to shift towards the AI-goblin narrative. Could get a large spike in attention if goblins in ai becomes an actual thing.
>Elien
Hinges on Elon Musk being an alien. Unlikely.
>Mirai
Doesn't have much going for it besides anime girl and low mcap, but is pretty dead without a working website.

I'm leaning towards gondola

62337941
>>62332874
kek shibbagie

62338221
>>62337913
>Hinges on Elon Musk being an alien. Unlikely.

not literally, just depends on how hard he wants to push the meme. theres lots of people that believe themselves to be alien souls despite having explicitly human dna, i think he fits that category. stranger still, theres people that report having contact and conversations with aliens where they confirm such things. some keep this information private because of the social repercussions for reporting on such experiences.

if aliens were a relevant topic in the age of tesla, we could imagine he might think himself as one. his mind so completely foreign to what we know as human.

62338233
>>62332874
How about Nerva/XNV? It's truly decentralized, can only be solo-mined via CPU, and is private. It doesn't match the meme theme of SHIB but I'd argue it's got even more going for it than SHIB did...

62338294
>>62337913
there is also bobo on eth

62338298
MUMU fits the bill.

On ETH
Solid token distribution
Active X and TG
Supported by other 4chan coin communities

0x2F573070E6090b3264Fe707e2C9F201716F123c7

62338308
>>62338294
Mumu & Bobo.

So apparently there is a split between ETH and SOL communities. BOBO and MUMU recently migrated to SOL, leaving the rest of the ETH die hard communities to fend for themselves.

MUMU/ BOBO/ WOJAK/ SNIBBU/ DODO/ APU along with PEPE still have strong communities on ETH.

62338319
>>62338233
Grabbed a bag and already up lol

62338327
>>62338308
>Mumu & Bobo.
The liquidity for Mumu on eth is garbage, but bobo has some strong liquidity. $142k of v2 burned. Snibbu has a decent amount of burned v2 liquidity, too. Same with dodo. Why did they all move to sol?

also op is a fag

62338664
>>62338327
From what I understand, the communities believe the volume for meme coins are on SOL, and Supply control was another factor.

I still think ETH is the home of MEMES and real money.

I've yet to see a buy on any meme coin on sol that made me say "Impressive".

62339678
>>62335171
I like Gondola but the Coingecko has no website or social info. Need to fix that

62339697
If we can't find a good one maybe we can have a tech savy anon spin up a shitter with a website, tg and x acc and a generous dev wallet for the effort. Maybe some good old tax system that gets distributed to holders. Would do it myself but too much of a brainlet

62339720
>>62338298
>MUMU
Yeah that shit is dead retard. Only high cap memes have a chance of pumping with btc every cycle. low cap shitters are one and done.

62339814
>>62339678
I just checked, the X account, TG, TikTok accounts are all on there. Even the sol wormhole contract is linked. Only thing missing on CG is the website. I guess someone could send them a message to add the website if it really matters. CMC has everything, including the wormhole and website listed, and has the "tokenomics" verified by CMC.

62339825
>>62339814
The cmc links to website goes to expired domain and the link to x goes to an acc which is legit buyt hasnt posted in a long time, other than that's it's quite solid actually. meme is good but mc could be a bit lower.

62339847
Honestly I would be down to gondolapost on X or whatever
I don't have a lot of gondolas saved but it's a comfy meme

62339879
>>62339825
The account posted an hour ago...? They have some old post pinned at the top, maybe that's what you're seeing? But yes, it'd be nice if the mc was lower for better entry. I'll take it as a trade off for having all the paid update shit done. And I don't know what's wrong with the website. Everything was working yesterday when I checked all the links. Maybe a hosting server issue.

62339945
fuck it, i'll put a couple hundred on gondola
love that lil spurdo

item.title: ICP — 35 replies — 13.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
ICP

Open thread • Replies: 35 • Updated: 2026-06-09 11:36 UTC

OP

>we have steve jobs at home

Early replies (first 3)

62336952
>>62336934
soon

62337106
>>62336934
ICP has the best tech

62337150
>>62337106
ICUP

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62337255
>>62336934
Dom is next Bill Gates but not a freak. ICP is new paradigm (yes I know meme word but it is unironically true) on same level as the PC

62337283
>>62337255
It's funny you say cause Bill gates is just a parasite who sucks off value and gives people aids, yeah you may be right, he is the next rat gates

62337316
>>62337236
I remember in 2000s when Amazon was down 99% in stocks while they were fully operational and people just like you tried to demoralize investors.

62337352
>>62337255
Bill Gates fucked a Russian hooker. Honestly based

62337356
>>62337316
It's been 6 years, faggot, 6 years and nothing to show for it, amazon gave back some profits post crash after 3 years, nobody is profitable on this piece of shit

62337365
>>62337356
>nothing to show for it

1. ICP was launched 5 years ago not 5
2. Caffeine.AI subscriptions are up 30% this month.
3 Partnerships with the UN, Switzerland, Pakistan, HBAR, Maestro etc..
4. Chain fusion

62337372
>>62337356
UN

62337376
>>62337316
I am ICP Chad but let’s not lie. Amazon went down ~90% after Dotcom crash and only once. ICP has gone down ~90% many times over (scam pump followed by crash) and is now down ~99% from ath. ICP is being repressed 10x more than Amazon because it is 10x a bigger deal

62337385
>>62337376
That’s because you’re ill informed. $700 was never the ATH, it was more like $80.
Hunchbacks used futures to manipulate the price at launch. That $700 literally lasted for a few hours before they deliberately crashed it.

62337420
>>62337376
Forget back -90%, nobody profited from this since Moonman shilled this around $50, the last semi good pump was from $3 to $20, that was the last pump, the somehow tech couldn't keep any pump because the rat of switzerland just keeps extracting, OGs were raped, early adopters never got anything back

62337568
>>62337420
Your obsession with moonman is actually a mental illness.
ICP is now far more advanced and mature than it was in 2024 when we reached almost $20.
The next pump will be much more extreme because ICP has had many adoptions this year.
You need to stop fudding, it’s really not healthy for you.

62337596
>>62337420
Also, mission 70 already cut inflation by 40%. The tokenomics are much better now than when ICP was worth $50. We just need an alt season to get this party going.

62337713
>>62337372
>icy pee is partnered with the foremost advocate of white genocide
bearish

62337760
>>62336934
I am considering dumping 10k into this. It might fail spectacularly but it’s the lowest MC and highest potential coin I can think of. I am tired of working myself to death.

62337787
>>62336934
Dis-lui que je lui passe le bonjour

62337830
>>62337760
That’s a good mindset. Worst case scenario you lose a few thousand dollars or you make half a million when the AI alt season starts.

62337832
>>62337385
I understand the futures Sam Barkman scam. I was going off a $200 top. Even $80 top puts ICP down 97% however

62337836
>>62337760
10k is a good gamble for something that has potential to retire you, just don’t over extend because the risk is big. ICP is imo still the most exciting narrative besides chainlink in crypto right now

62337916
>>62337836
Chainlink is not that exciting lol

62337929
>>62336934
Lol the black turtleneck lives on

62337937
>>62337760
For your own good, don't, trust me, i've been suckered into this scam for 6 years, it has fundamental issues stemming from it's core team, they are ok with the price hovering low just surviving and extracting

62337958
>>62337787
are you talking about mm?

62338026
>>62337937
I’ve held on and off for years as well. But seems like things are starting to click now.

62338066
>>62338026
Don’t listen to him, he’s a known fudder on biz, he hated ICP for years, his obsession with moonman is a mental illness.

62338597
>>62337916
Chainlink is very exciting, they research so much

62338901
>>62336934

62339051
>>62336934
>his first name on his handle is in lower caps
Woah these zoomers are so young and hip. I bet he's like 21 or something.

62339665
please fuck my ass

62339717
>>62337372
Looks like the entire top 30 is in that same list. What the fuck are you doing.

62340134
>>62339717
Dfinity is the most important because they have a partnership with the government of Pakistan a country with 100 million people.

item.title: Nano thread? Nano thread. XNO — 15 replies — 12.8/hr

Open thread • Replies: 15 • Updated: 2026-06-09 13:00 UTC

OP

Early replies (first 3)

62332177
wait why does every nano thread get deleted?

62332215
>>62332177
Has any coin been more suppressed on biz before? Every single mention of XNO or nano is deleted. This is real and documentable. Military tribunals are needed.

62332418
>>62332082
>>62332177
>>62332215
BUY NANO SAAR

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62332431
>>62332082
I don't wanna buy Nano.

62333240
>>62332418
>>62332431
hey watch your fucking mouth.

62333599
nano has won already, by design

why don't you fucking get that?! Huh??

62333666
>>62332082
What is point of these boomer pow coins? Like why Ltc, doge coin, nano or dash? Like who the fuck cares about transaction coins like this. So stupid

62335399
>>62333666
it's not PoW albeit, hence why nano has the best speeds at 0 fees

62335843
>>62332215
Except for all the spam you post in xsg.
And this thread.

62336007
>>62335843
Those posts all get deleted

>>62335878
You will answer in front of a military tribunal for crimes against humanity before this is over.

62337016
>>62336007
Yes, military tribunals are coming. Justice will be served.

62339656
nano is better than your coin

62340117
>>62339656
>>62332082
Nano is literally a double exit scam that already dumped. This is like buying UST after the second pretend at revitalization pump when it dumped everything. You lost on this one. We all have one we messed up on. Take your licks and move on rather than shilling your bags to cope. Stop wasting time and missing the only opportunity you will ever have at this scale and buy better coins.

62340336
>>62340117
You thought you could suppress the apex coin of humanity and just walk away? Nobody walks away from this.

You WILL face the military tribunals.

item.title: NUCLEAR HAPPENING — 12 replies — 9.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
NUCLEAR HAPPENING

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-06-09 12:54 UTC

OP

CLAUDE MYTHOS AUDIT SHOWED NO SERIOUS SECURITY ISSUES FOR THE BITCOIN NETWORK

WHO THE FUCK EVEN CREATED THIS IMPENETRABLE SOFTWARE?

Early replies (first 3)

62339899
>Mythos
so a scam, ok got it

62339900
its because the bitcoin developers dont add anything, the code has barely changed.

honestly it's quite mysterious.

62339947
no shit nigga it's like 600 lines of Dino code

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62339953
epstein

62340131
it's a more of a protocol than a software
keep it simple and nothing breaks. make a solid foundation before you build the empire state building on top of it, ect.

62340138
But a lot of defi protocols will 100% get exploited in the coming weeks

62340183
>>62339891
source? no search engine results

62340196
It's actually that bitcoin is too simple, the more complexity you add the more likely you get a vulnerability.

62340251
>>62340131
Robert Kiyosaki? You read rich dad poor dad?

62340255
>>62340196
Too simple how?

62340280
>>62339891
>security researchers find out when you dont add backdoors in everything there are no security issues

62340317
>>62340255
It's not got any smart contracts or other bullshit tacked onto it
